Identical#

attacks.identical.bulyan(grad_stck, grad_avg, target_idx=-1, **kwargs)[source]#

Compute the attack vector adapted from “The Hidden Vulnerability”. :param target_idx Index of the targeted coordinate: :param “all” for all:

See:

make_attack

attacks.identical.check(grad_honests, f_real, defense, factor=-16, negative=False, **kwargs)[source]#

Check parameter validity for this attack template. :param grad_honests Non-empty list of honest gradients: :param f_real Number of Byzantine gradients to generate: :param defense Aggregation rule in use to defeat: :param … Ignored keyword-arguments:

Returns:

Whether the given parameters are valid for this attack

attacks.identical.empire(grad_stck, grad_avg, **kwargs)[source]#

Compute the attack vector adapted from “Fall of Empires”. See:

make_attack

attacks.identical.func(grad_stck, grad_avg, **kwargs)#

Compute the attack vector adapted from “A Little is Enough”. See:

make_attack

attacks.identical.little(grad_stck, grad_avg, **kwargs)[source]#

Compute the attack vector adapted from “A Little is Enough”. See:

make_attack

attacks.identical.make_attack(compute_direction)[source]#

Make the attack gradient generation closure associated with an attack direction. :param compute_direction Attack vector computation: :type compute_direction Attack vector computation: stacked honest gradients, average honest gradient, forwarded keyword-arguments…) -> attack vector (in the gradient space, no reference :param : :type : stacked honest gradients, average honest gradient, forwarded keyword-arguments…) -> attack vector (in the gradient space, no reference

Returns:

Byzantine gradient generation closure
